compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## DONE + live-verified (2026-07-01)
Landed via Gerrit change 123 (-> GitHub main d96628c9).
- infra/terraform-github/main.tf: main-only (tag_lock removed), check block -> fail-closed
  lifecycle precondition on main_lock. Stale 18048287 comment reconciled (actual legacy deleted:
  18306946).
- Live reconcile: terraform init (S3 backend) + terraform import github_repository_ruleset.main_lock
  rebar:18402431; 'terraform plan' -> 'No changes. Your infrastructure matches the configuration.'
  (re-verified clean after the a774 drift test).
- terraform validate: Success. Contractual test tests/unit/test_mirror_lock_terraform.py (3 tests):
  main-only, precondition-not-check, full lock contract. All pass.

compiled_state.description:
## What
Reconcile infra/terraform-github with the live GitHub mirror-lock and make the lock's
deploy-key existence gate FAIL-CLOSED. The lock was applied via gh-api during WS7 (ruleset
gerrit-mirror-lock-main, id 18402431); Terraform state is empty and the module still defines a
tag_lock we deliberately did NOT apply (main-only cutover).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] infra/terraform-github/main.tf is MAIN-ONLY: the tag_lock resource + its output are removed
      (tags stay human-pushable so release.yml v* pushes keep working).
- [ ] The check "deploy_key_present" block is replaced by a lifecycle { precondition } on the
      main_lock resource so a MISSING replication deploy key ABORTS apply (fail-closed), not just WARNs.
- [ ] Stale comments reconciled: the deleted legacy ruleset was id 18306946 (not 18048287); note the
      main-only decision.
- [ ] Live reconcile: terraform import of the live ruleset (rebar:18402431) into main_lock, then
      terraform plan reports "No changes" against live GitHub.
- [ ] terraform validate passes; a contractual test asserts the config is main-only + has the
      precondition (no tag_lock).

## Files
- infra/terraform-github/main.tf
compiled_state.title: Terraform-github state reconcile + drift detection for the mirror-lock ruleset
